query: Mattel July 2024 first blind Barbie American Foundation for the Blind black Barbie Down syndrome

mako.raw:
{"query":"Mattel July 2024 first blind Barbie American Foundation for the Blind black Barbie Down syndrome","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1649,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://ndss.org/barbie-ndss","title":"Barbie® Dolls with Down syndrome | National Down Syndrome Society (NDSS)","content":"On April 25, 2023, Barbie® introduced its first doll with Down syndrome, a step to further increasing disability representation in the toy aisle.
Barbie once again partnered with NDSS to introduce its next doll with Down syndrome to the Barbie Fashionistas 2024 lineup."},{"url":"https://afb.org/professionalservices/case-studies/blind-barbie","title":"Mattel Introduces First Blind Barbie Fashionista Doll in Partnership with AFB | American Foundation for the Blind","content":"On July 23, 2024, Barbie announced its first Blind Barbie Fashionista Doll, created to allow even more children to find a doll that represents them and inspire all children to tell more stories through play.
Barbie partnered with the American Foundation for the Blind (AFB) to ensure all details of the blind Barbie doll, including face sculpt, fashion, accessories, packaging experience, and e-commerce communication, accurately depict an individual with blindness or low vision."},{"url":"https://mashable.com/article/mattel-new-blind-barbie-disability-representation","title":"Mattel unveils first blind Barbie in new diverse doll launch | Mashable","content":""By increasing representation and promoting awareness about the experiences of blind individuals and those with low vision, our shared goal is to inspire boundless opportunities for everyone, ultimately leading to a world of greater accessibility and inclusion," wrote American Foundation for the Blind CEO Eric Bridges.
The two new dolls were designed in collaboration with the American Foundation for the Blind and the National Down syndrome Society, a previous Mattel creative partner.
"NDSS is thrilled to introduce a second Barbie doll with Down syndrome," wrote NDSS president and CEO Kandi Pickard."},{"url":"https://www.usatoday.com/story/money/2024/07/23/new-barbie-dolls-inclusive/74501032007/","title":"Mattel introduces blind Barbie, Black Barbie with Down syndrome","content":"Barbie has announced two new additions to its growing line of diverse and inclusive dolls: a blind Barbie and the first Black Barbie with Down syndrome.
Mattel announced the two new dolls on Tuesday as part of Barbie's 2024 Fashionistas Line, an inclusive range of dolls with over 175 varieties of skin tone, eye color, hair color and texture, body types, disabilities and fashions.
Both dolls will be available for purchase starting Tuesday and will also appear in the YouTube series, "Barbie Doll Adventures."
This Barbie is the second to be designed in collaboration with the National Down Syndrome Society (NDSS), though this time Mattel went a step further and also consulted a focus group of Black individuals from the Down syndrome community.
Having this doll launched alongside the new Barbie doll with Blindness marks another important step in expanding representationfor the disability community,” said Kandi Pickard, President and CEO of NDSS in a press release.
The Barbie Fashionistas line was first introduced in 2009 and releases a new lineup of dolls each year."}]}
